candidates.stmt: I am an NHS clinician and local community champion with two decades of roots in Milton Keynes. I am standing for the Liberal Democrats to continue my lifelong commitment to public service.

Qualifications and experience:
Parish councillor ( West Bletchley).
PhD - Manufacturing Operations research.
Expert in Continuous Improvement.
Top priorities:
Protect high street and local traders.
Speeding and parking issues.
Keeping Green spaces clean and safe.
Increase social/ affordable houses.
Support youth activities.

I have lived with my family in Milton Keynes for over 14 years. I'm a semi-retired HR Consultant with an interest in encouraging fair employment practices. 
As Milton Keynes expands and develops as a city I am passionate about ensuring that city standards are upheld as well as possible while keeping council tax low, with examples including: 
- the condition of our roads and redways, 
- green spaces protected or provided for, 
- our environment is free of litter and graffiti, 
- our commitment to waste management is maintained; and
-there is sufficient affordable housing.

Overall I want Milton Keynes to be a city we can always be proud to live in, and a place where people and organisations are attracted to come and settle in. 
I am an active member of the local BCG cycling club, keen walker, gardener and student of politics and current affairs.

A retired engineer with 32 years in the offshore industry; I moved to Bletchley 3 years ago. I want to work with residents to improve the area to ensure Council Tax is well spent, fix potholes properly, tackle graffiti, fly-tipping and antisocial behaviour. Let’s reform Bletchley!

Here's my election points for Bletchley and Buckingham . We need regeneration of housing estates and high streets.  Small and medium businesses need tax breaks and help. Vital voluntary services need funding not scrapping.  Mental health , care in the community and homecare services need to be a priority.  In this case both Milton Keynes and Buckinghamshire councils need to be more proactive and transparent with regards to all consultation and decisions.  Planning departments need to make sure infrastructure is in place while houses and flats are being built . Social services both children's and adult need to be more clear and concise with advocates and appropriate adults when dealing with the vulnerable.  Roads need to be kept and not left to rack and ruin .  Police and crime commissioner needs to be more open and proactive with all elected officials and again , open consultation.  Stop and search needs to be a normal thing .  All crime needs to be investigated .  With a budget of over £850 million it needs to in invested into more police on the beat .  Council needs to go back to employing a DLBO rather than contractors.   GP surgeries need to have a liason for proper communication with their patients.  Social housing has to become a priority,  that includes a major slowdown of evictions by private landlords regardless .  Schools need to be able to provide more one to one for SEN children and teenagers . Wheelchair and and disabled housing needs to be doubled . And the MP'S need to be able to have much more influence within the council and their decision making . Salaries for CEO's need to be capped , earning more than the prime minister is outrageous.   Council staff in every department need to have at least 3 - 5 years experience . Council offices are a public building  , they need to be accessible to the public . A second hospital has been spoken about for years , it needs to be built.  Immigration has to kirbed . Manufacturing has to be stepped up . Education

Kerrie Bradburn was born and raised in Milton Keynes and is now bringing up her two daughters here. She cares deeply about the area and the people who live here, both as a local councillor and as a mum. First elected in 2019, Kerrie is known for being approachable, hardworking and quick to get things done. She played a leading role in helping Milton Keynes introduce its new recycling system and now chairs the council’s Environment and Place Scrutiny Committee. In her day job, Kerrie works as Exams Manager at Hazeley Academy.

Marie has lived in Milton Keynes for over 30 years and was first elected in 2016. She served as Mayor of Milton Keynes in 2024 to 2025, representing the city with warmth, dedication and pride.

As a councillor, Marie is known for her caring and determined approach to casework, especially supporting elderly residents with complex issues and making sure they are not left to navigate difficult systems alone. She is also a strong champion for active travel and cycling, helping to make it easier and safer for people to get around Milton Keynes.

Marie is passionate about creating opportunities for young people and has been a consistent advocate for children in care, foster carers and kinship carers, working to make sure their voices are heard and that every child and family gets the support they need.

Having lived in Milton Keynes for 27 years and run a small business, I'm standing to give our community a stronger voice, cut wasteful spending, fix roads, and ensure planning rules are respected. I will focus on safer streets, reliable services, and protect what makes our area special, putting residents first

**Building a Better Bradwell for Everyone**

I have called Milton Keynes home for the last 8 years. Like many of you, I moved here for the opportunity, the community, and the chance to build a bright future for my family. As a software professional and a business owner, I believe in looking at problems logically and finding efficient, common-sense solutions.
I am standing for the local council because I believe that local government should serve every resident equally. Whether you have lived here for decades or have recently made Bradwell your home, your concerns are my priorities.

**My Commitment to You:**

*Safe & Accessible Streets:* From fixing potholes to improving lighting, I will ensure our infrastructure is safe and reliable for all commuters, parents, and elderly residents.

*Fiscal Accountability:* I am committed to ensuring your council tax is spent wisely on the services that actually matter to our ward, cutting out waste and bureaucracy.

*A United Community:* I will be a visible, approachable representative who listens to every voice, ensuring that our diverse neighbourhood is a place where everyone feels they belong and are heard.

*Support for Local Families:* I will advocate for better local amenities and cleaner, greener public spaces for all generations to enjoy.

*I am asking for your vote.* I don’t just want to represent a ward; I want to represent our shared aspiration for a safer, more prosperous, and well-managed community.

🗳️ Election Information
Candidate: KOYADA, Gowri Shankar
Ward: Bradwell Ward
Date: Thursday, 7th May

**Let’s work together for a stronger Bradwell. Every vote counts!**

•Prioritising clean, safe streets for everyone
•Tackling litter, fly-tipping, and anti-social behaviour
•Supporting visible, responsive local policing and safety initiatives
•Organising regular community meet-ups and local events
•Improving communication between councillors and residents
•Making it easier for you to be heard and involved in decisions
•Strengthening community spirit and neighbourhood pride
•Encouraging connection, inclusion, and community cohesion
•Being approachable, accountable, and active in the community

In 2026, Wing Tsang is standing as a Liberal Democrat candidate for Broughton and Moulsoe Ward, covering Broughton, Broughton Gate, Brooklands and Moulsoe, in the 7 May Milton Keynes City Council local election.

Wing was born and raised in Milton Keynes and comes from a long-standing local family business, giving her a clear understanding of the community and how the city has developed. She is a Chartered Marketer and a member of the Chartered Institute of Public Relations (MCIPR), with a professional background in communications, marketing and strategic planning across multiple sectors.

Wing is multilingual, speaking English and Cantonese, which allows her to connect with a wider range of residents and ensure more people feel heard and included in local conversations.

In her professional career, Wing has worked with organisations to improve how they communicate, engage communities and deliver clear, practical outcomes. She currently serves as Vice Chair of the Chartered Institute of Marketing South East, supporting regional professionals and helping to shape industry standards.

Wing is actively involved in community work, including volunteering with a campaign tackling racism and supporting initiatives that bring people together. She focuses on practical local priorities, including protecting green spaces, improving road conditions and making streets safer.

Wing is community-minded, puts residents first and takes action on the issues that matter locally. She is visible in Broughton and Moulsoe, speaking to residents, reporting issues and working to get them resolved.

Vote for Wing Tsang, Liberal Democrat Focus Team on 7 May.

I'm a parish councillor for Broughton and Moulsoe and for the Greens I Chair the committee meetings, help with campaigns, and manage the website.

I'm also a software developer and write science fiction novels on the side.

**Transparency**

We need government that is as transparent and accountable as possible. I don't trust anyone with any kind of power, and neither should you.

**Public engagement**

Our engagement with the government should not stop at the ballot box. It should be ongoing. Beyond anything else, we can help! The people of Milton Keynes want to help their neighbours, so we should be able to give proper input on decisions, and events should be put on to engage with the residents.

**Transfer of powers and funding to parish councils**

Parish councils are- or should be- filled with people who know the local area and its needs. They can be in contact with the locals in a way the city council can't. They should be doing as much as possible, and given the funding needed.

**Local ownership**

Franchised businesses destroy the character of an area, drain it of its resources, and leads to those working and shopping there feeling disconnected from the business. We should support local businesses.

Hello Milton Keynes residents, I have lived and worked in and around the Milton Keynes Broughton area for over a decade.

Milton Keynes is a wonderful example of garden city, but we desperately need a change from the old parties which have let us down again and again. I am delighted to be standing for the Heritage Party in the local borough and parish elections in Milton Keynes Central to offer residents a common-sense candidate choice. As a concerned resident in the Broughton area, I know what needs to be done to improve our community. My priorities are set out below.

GET THE DEVELOPMENT UNDER CONTROL Milton Keynes has already had significant development imposed on it at great pace and the local services in term of GP, hospital, school, public transport and road traffic are under severe stress. The Heritage Party will fight to keep our countryside green and prevent any more prime agricultural land from being concreted over. Especially in Milton Keynes where the “Garden City” principles are being water down due to over development.

PROTECT OUR FARMS against green belt over development and legal barriers preventing family farms from being transmitted to the next generation. No farmers, no local good quality food. Instead of subsidising giant international farming corporation for not producing food, we should increase subsidies to become self-sufficient in food while promoting regenerative and organic farming.

PROTECT OUR CHILDREN In Milton Keynes and other Councils around the country, Comprehensive Sexuality Education has been introduced from primary school. School children are being exposed to appalling lesson content that confuses them about gender and promote sexual experimentation at a very young age. I will intervene to protect the innocence of our children.

LET’S KEEP MILTON KEYNES SAFE The job of the police is to protect our community against criminals. I’ll intervene to make sure the police are doing their proper job and get more foot patrols, rather than

Having lived in MK for over 35yrs I understand the issues affecting the ward of Willen and Campbell Park. As a retired police officer who now has his own business in the area I know what value for money and Public service means. The city needs real change and a return to the vision it once was. The merry go round of the old the party system isnt delivering the services you deserve for the area or MK. If elected I will be visible, accessible and your voice for the ward.

A retired Police officer and Military Veteran, I moved to Milton Keynes a few years ago. I can see we urgently need to change how our city is run. 
Potholes, anti social behaviour, over expansion, funds wasted on vanity projects must be fixed. Your Vote counts. Let’s Reform MK together.

**About**

I was born in Kenya and moved to Milton Keynes as a child in 1976, when the city was still in its earliest stages of development. Growing up alongside Milton Keynes, I’ve seen first-hand how our community has evolved—and I even played a small part in its story, helping to create one of our famous concrete cows.

My career has been rooted in community and creativity. Throughout the 1980s and 90s, I worked to support and develop the arts in Milton Keynes, alongside involvement in youth work, counselling, and initiatives tackling homelessness across our city.

Coming from a family background in human rights and constitutional law, I’ve always believed in putting people at the heart of decision-making. I am committed to bringing that human-centred approach into local government—advocating for better council services, fairer planning, and housing that truly meets the needs of residents.

The pandemic revealed both the strength of our communities and the deep inequalities that persist. Like many, I became disillusioned with everyday politics—but that changed when I heard Zack Polanski speak. It renewed my sense of hope and purpose, and inspired me to stand as a local candidate to help turn that hope into real change for Milton Keynes.

**Campaign Priorities**

Milton Keynes’ greatest strength is its diversity—of culture, ideas, and people. As your councillor, I will work to harness these strengths to build a fairer, more inclusive city.

I will stand up to the negative impacts of developer-led housing by pushing for genuinely affordable homes and protecting the integrity of our communities. Tackling inequality—locally and across the city—will be central to everything I do, alongside improving how residents access and experience council services to ensure better outcomes for everyone, especially those most marginalised.

I am passionate about creating more opportunities for young people. By supporting arts, sports, and community initiatives—and working closely w

**About**

After first walking around Willen Lake and seeing the Peace Pagoda, I instantly decided Milton Keynes was the place I wanted to live. Now entering my 6th year of residence, I really appreciate the perfect balance of nature and innovation the city thrives on.

Even though this is my first foray into the political world, I am highly adaptable and love to learn. Professionally, I am a software developer with an interest in writing, games (board and virtual), films, and sports.

**Campaign Priorities**

Honestly, priority number one is trying to stop those who perpetuate hate from gaining any more power. While this local election should focus on local issues, national politics will always cast a shadow over proceedings.

To that end, I am standing as a quieter, more empathetic protest vote for people wishing to express their displeasure at our current government.

This election is unique, where you have three votes, so if you are at all Green-curious, I would strongly recommend using at least one of your votes for Soraya. She is an excellent candidate. If you have another vote to spare then Roy is also a fantastic option.

If you want to use all three votes on Green candidates and I somehow ended up a councillor, my main focus will be around sustainability and ethical technological integration.

I will champion a platform for **Real Energy Sovereignty: MK Energy for MK Residents** - We should utilise the natural resources Milton Keynes has around us to heat and power as many Milton Keynes homes as we can. I will encourage community climate resilience, protecting ourselves against the instability of geopolitics in order to finally control spiralling gas and energy bills.

To achieve this ambition and more, I have the following proposals:

- Heat Willen Hospice with heat pumps from Willen Lake.
- Heat homes with heat pumps from the Grand Union Canal.
- Encourage the dual-use of our parking spaces by installing solar canopies over them to generate power for EVs

**About**

I moved to Milton Keynes four years ago, drawn here by the Redways. As a keen cyclist this system excited me with the promise of freedom, eventually leading me to a car free life which I couldn’t have done without them.  I work for an engineering company delivering renewable energy and sustainable packaging projects, so I’m heavily involved in the circular economy, which I believe should be a priority for everyone in their everyday life.
I'll be honest, I haven't always been politically engaged. But watching the growing disconnect between the people who live in our communities and the decisions being made on their behalf pushed me to stop waiting for someone else to step up.

**Campaign Priorities**

I'm standing because I believe everyone deserves a voice in shaping the place they call home. Milton Keynes is a brilliant, diverse city with incredible green spaces and infrastructure that other places can only dream of, but it needs people who will fight to protect and improve what we have, not take it for granted.
My priorities are ensuring our Redways are properly maintained and safe for everyone, pushing for inclusive and affordable public transport, and bringing my professional experience in renewables and the circular economy to bear on local decision-making. I stand with Hope Not Hate and believe our diversity is one of our greatest strengths. At the end of the day, I care about people, all people, and I think that's the bare minimum we should expect from anyone who wants to represent us.

Paul has worked for the NHS for 17 years and proudly served Central Milton Keynes as a councillor for eight years. Now, after leaving the Labour Party, he’s standing for the Green Party in the community where he grew up.

I have lived in Oldbrook for 30 years and served as a Parish Councillor since 2014. My main concerns are planning and road safety. 
My successes on the Parish Council include having two unregistered HMO's closed, closure of the right turn into Oldbrook across a dual carriageway on the V6, and the reduction to 40mph of the speed limit on H7 by the schools opposite Oldbrook. An ex-FA licensed football coach and referee, I believe in fairness for all. I want to be a City Councillor to use my experience for the good of the local residents.

Toni is a software engineering manager who has lived in Milton Keynes for 5 years. He brings a "delivery-first" mindset to local politics and is committed to using his professional experience in execution and planning to tackle the state of our roads, the housing crisis, and the pressures on our local NHS. Toni wants to see a move away from generic retail chains and see CMK thrive as a hub for unique, local businesses.

Johnny Luk lives in Glebe Farm with his wife in the ward of Danesborough in Milton Keynes. He started his career in the civil service in the Fast Stream Leadership Programme and eventually worked in four government departments. He has worked in business and policy and was a former university governor.

Outside of work, he was a two time parliamentary candidate in 2019 and 2024, was a National Schools Gold Medalist in rowing in 2009 and enjoys writing in his spare time (including about rowing!). He hopes to use his energy and policy experience to improve the way our local government works and ensure it is focused on serving residents.

My name is Anthony Fabrizio, I am proud to say I have lived in the area since 1996, 30 years, I felt it was time to stand as a Reform UK candidate in this local election. I am putting myself forward because I believe our community deserves practical solutions, honest representation, and a stronger voice in the decisions that affect our daily lives.
One of my key priorities is improving how local planning decisions are made. Too often, developments move forward without proper consideration of infrastructure, community impact, or residents’ concerns. I would like for our green and historic area of Milton Keynes have a more common sense approach to planning so it is fair, transparent, and genuinely reflects the needs of all the people who live here in our area, Little Brickhill, Bow Brickhill, Woburn Sands and some of our newer areas that have been developed without adequate infrastructure investment.
Transport is another MAJOR issue facing our area. With changes to the RAILWAY , the ROADS, increasing pressure on stations such as Woburn Sands and Brickhill, and our village roads, we need better coordination, accessibility, and long-term thinking. I will push for improvements that make commuting easier, safer, and more reliable for everyone.
Our roads are also in urgent need of attention. Potholes are more than just a nuisance—they are a safety hazard and a cost burden for drivers. I will advocate for more consistent maintenance and faster repairs to keep our roads in proper condition.
Access to healthcare is a growing concern. Many residents are struggling to secure timely GP appointments or find NHS dentists. I will work with local authorities and health providers to improve access and ensure that basic healthcare services are available when people need them.
Safer streets for our youth and opportunities for our younger generations.
Finally, I am committed to supporting families and young people through better school placements and opportunities. Every child deserves

I am a long-time Milton Keynes resident, who wants better value for council tax, improved
local services, and safer streets. A former local football club chairman dedicated to youth and
community, I’m standing for election to make residents’ voices heard and deliver real
improvements.
I would appreciate your vote

I have lived in Milton Keynes (Kents Hill & Monkston) for 32 years and have watched this side of the city turn into the vibrant area it is today.
I work as an environmental auditor and have a keen interest in recycling and sustainable living.
I am keen to see Milton Keynes maintain its position as having one of the highest recycling rates in England and, if elected, I would also like to work on improving public transport in this area of the city.

Having lived in Shenley Brook End in Milton Keynes since 1999 I am very aware of the issues residents face. I recently retired from a council position as Head of Project Services so now want to implement significant improvement to our failing services and properly manage the repair to our crumbling roads.

Having lived in MK all my life, I love this city deeply. I've campaigned to protect and improve MK's parks, grid roads, and other unique features for 20 years. If elected, I will push for the city's infrastructure to be improved. That means upgrading, not downgrading, our grid roads. It means fixing potholes and broken redways. It means maintaining verges and lighting. I will also push for new developments and infrastructure changes to be respectful of both the city's heritage and the character of our older towns and villages.

The city I love has been neglected for too long. It's time to reverse the decline.

My party’s local manifesto is at:
milton-keynes-Labour-party.paperturn-view.com/a-succesful-milton-keynes-for-everyone?pid=ODk8936620&v=1.1

If you’re tired of empty promises, you’re not alone.

Ten years as a parish councillor. Proven results. I’ve lived here since 1978 and delivered locally.

I’m standing again to demand better for residents and build on what works. No spin. No nonsense. Just results.

26 Years of Loyalty. Now, I’m putting it to Work for You.
I’ve lived in Milton Keynes for 26 years. I’ve held the same job for 26 years. I’ve raised my children in our schools for 26 years.

In a world of "here today, gone tomorrow," I am the candidate who stays. I don't just talk about commitment; I live it. Now, I’m asking for the opportunity to bring that same lifelong loyalty to our Council.

Safety Without Compromise: My number.one priority is the safety of women and girls in our community. Words aren't enough—we need action.

Brightening the Redways: Our Redways are the heart of MK, but they shouldn't be dark or intimidating. I will fight for better lighting and increased police visibility so we can all walk our city with confidence.

A Real Local Voice: I’m not a career politician. I’m your neighbour. I will ensure your concerns are heard—loudly and clearly.

No spin. No nonsense. Just common sense.

Sophie Bell is an Archaeological Surveyor and Heritage Consultant with a strong commitment to supporting her local community. In 2023, Sophie was elected as a Milton Keynes City Councillor for Shenley Brook End ward, and moved into Great Linford ward last year. She serves as Vice-Chair of the Corporate Parenting Panel and as Planning Spokesperson. Sophie also played a key role in pushing for a review of SEND provision in Milton Keynes through the SEND Task and Finish Group. Her passion is improving play space for children in the ward.

Perry Morgan has lived in Milton Keynes since 2000 and went to Stantonbury Campus, so he knows the Great Linford ward really well and now calls it home. He has a strong public service background, having worked as a Police Officer and supported charities like the Prison Advice Care Trust. Today, he works in safeguarding, supporting young people and managing risk, using his experience to help keep the community safe, strong and together; something that’s at the heart of his vision for Great Linford.

I'm a long-term Great Linford resident and have lived here in some capacity for nearly 28 years. I moved to Milton Keynes when I was 3 years-old and have grown up with the city as it has grown and evolved too over the subsequent 34 years I have lived here.

I first got involved in local politics at young age when I was 15 and have represented Milton Keynes before as its Member of Youth Parliament in 2007, later on I was a students' union officer and in 2025 became a parish councillor on Great Linford Parish Council, representing its Great Linford ward. I have volunteered for many local charities and organisations over the years. Currently I am a trustee of a local arts charity that serves communities Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire and Bedfordshire.

Most of my professional background was working in the arts/entertainment sector until I unexpectedly had to become an unpaid carer for several years. During this time retrained as a bookkeeper and now own my own small business based here in Great Linford and hope to expand and employ local staff in the future. I recently also began some additional studies in local government finance that I hope to make use of in public service in future both on the parish council and if elected, as a ward councillor.

I'm standing as an independent because personally I feel local politics is more important than national politics and far more visible results can actually be achieved at this level and at the moment I don't really feel like I agree with what any of the major parties are offering locally. The main issues I'm standing on are:

- Restoring and upgrading our redways. Great Linford ward has some of the oldest and most poorly maintained sections of redway in Milton Keynes. I want to change this and want to see them restored and improved.

- Fixing our streetlights. The ward suffers from streetlight failures and aged/degraded cables in multiple places that is making it unsafe at night. While some work has been done I will fight to

Your local candidate. I live in Hanslope and have been it's City Councillor for 3 years. I've also been a Parish Councillor and held positions on the Rural Police & Crime Forum, Vice-Chair of Housing, Health and Adult Social Care Committee and other voluntary bodies.

I love where I live, love working here, and working hard. I've been working hard to improve our roads and pavements. Stop bad planning applications. And now working on making sure that the MK North proposed expansion does not go ahead.

I am a business women with a wide range of experience - insurance, motorsport, car industry, tech and more. I'm proud to call Milton Keynes home.

Our group has a manifesto - an actual plan for Milton Keynes - how to fix the basics and plan for the future.

I have my own pledges on my leaflets and you can see the things I've been working on and what I'm focussing on - detailed on my Facebook, Insta pages. And I'm availalbe by phone and email - hello@alison-andrew.co.uk or 09108 738777.

I am committed to improving and protecting our incredible Ward. You'll find me at many local events and always happy to meet for a beer or coffee!

After 35 years as a resident in the area running a farm and previous career as a Chartered Accountant, if elected, I’ll bring real world experience to serve our community of Hanslope.

A former lifelong Conservative voter I joined Reform in 2024 to drive the change Britain needs to work to improve our community.

I am a Chartered Accountant and a former president of the local branch of the Institute of Chartered Accountants (ICAEW). I am also a Chartered Tax Adviser and a former chair of the local branch of the Chartered Institute of Taxation (CIOT).
From 2022 to 2025 I was chair of the Milton Keynes Liberal Democrats.

I have lived in Milton Keynes since 1996, working as a tax adviser, and lately as a tax journalist. I am now retired and spend nearly all of my time working to get good people elected to local or national government.

I moved to Milton Keynes with my wife and young family as it is such a fantastic place for children to grow up. I have found a great sense of community spirit and a place where many people are working hard to make sure our city can fulfil its potential. Whether that is making sure that we have clean and beautiful public spaces, or that our schools are run to the best standard that they can be, there is a great number of both professionals and volunteers striving to help others.

The city is facing a lot of challenges. The Council are seeing astonishing budget cuts which makes it hard to maintain crucial front line services, adult social care, child protection and helping the homeless whilst also giving us the cleaning and landscaping services we’ve all come to expect. In addition the communities we are all a part of are having to work harder than ever to remain connected.

Working together I believe that we can continue to see a strong Milton Keynes providing the services that the most in need require as well seeing thriving high streets, local communities and high quality schools for our children. I look forward to speaking to as many residents as possible to hear your thoughts on how this can achieved and working hard with the stakeholders to achieve this. I can’t wait to hear from you and to play whatever role I can in making our community the very best it can be. I hope to see you on the doorstep but if you want to reach me at anytime please email stephen@mklabour.org.uk or contact me on facebook @Stephen4BradwellWard.

I have lived in Milton Keynes for nearly 40 years. I understand the issues affecting residents. An engineer by trade, now retired, Im standing to represent you, root out waste and fund services important to you. New Bradwell needs Reform UK and together we can make the necessary changes.

I'm standing in my home ward to give voters the chance to make hope normal again by electing a Green Party Councilor for New Bradwell.

I've lived in New Bradwell since 2019, so I know the area and it's issues well. I work as a Postie for Royal Mail in Newport Pagnell, and serve as my delivery unit's trade union rep, so helping people resolve issues is something that I do every day. I also have political experience, having served as a Green parish councillor for several years. If elected, I will work tirelessly on behalf of the constituents of New Bradwell, and to promote Green Party policies in Council business. I am passionate about the fantastic green spaces we have in Milton Keynes, and will fight to preserve and improve them. Public transport is a lifeline for many people, and I will fight to improve services and to keep fares low. I believe that any new housing built in Milton Keynes should be future proofed against the volatile energy market by ensuring that it is properly insulated and includes solar panels and heat pumps.

A bit about me: I'm a Dad and a Grandad; love live music and walking, and can often be found in the Craufurd arms or one of our fantastic local pubs and independent breweries. I love living in Milton Keynes, and want to help to keep it a brilliant place to live. I would be honoured to serve as your councillor for New Bradwell.
I moved to Milton Keynes in 2009 to work for the OU. I want to improve education and training for all age groups in the city.

I am standing as a Reform UK candidate in Newport Pagnell. I have lived in the Newport Pagnell area for 12 years (and Buckinghamshire for over 38).
I have worked both in the public sector (NHS Pathology service), in large private sector organisations (such as IBM) and as a director of smaller enterprises both as an owner and otherwise.
I have previously served as a Councillor elsewhere in Buckinghamshire. Now retired, I wish to use this broad experience to improve service delivery to MK and ensure that taxpayer's money is spent as efficiently as possible.

living in the Olney & Rural Ward, I understand Local issues and how to get results with experience in the NHS, local government, property and engineering services.

If Elected, I will work hard to make MK city Council more accountable, visible and active as your counsellor, fighting for our fair share of resources”

I am generally very approachable and always try to listen to people with a view to help.

I have lived in Milton Keynes for 13 years. In that time I have served as a Great Linford Parish councillor for 2 years and have championed; armed forces, mental health, and disability.
One of my main passions is creating space and support for children and youth, creating positive change to help guide our young people to obtain better skills and work opportunities. 
Having been a youth leader for many years I know that change can be made by just listening and making a safer active environment for the young of our community to thrive. 
In my years of experience of volunteering within many charities, giving and understanding goes a long way.

I have lived in the Milton Keynes area all my life, being brought up in Newport Pagnell in a family with very local roots.  
I am a Newport Pagnell Town councillor with 4 years of experience helping residents on local issues. I am also an active volunteer with many charities and not-for-profit organisations and I’m not afraid to get stuck in where it matters and get my hands dirty. 
I care greatly for the local community of all creeds and ages.

Jenni has lived in Milton Keynes for over 40 years and has been a Councillor or Parish Councillor in Monkston and Monkston Park for 22 years. She has specialised in housing and planning on the City Council.  She’s got the Council to demand more family homes from developers and to include affordable homes to buy as well as to rent in future developments. She helped get funding for a feasibility study for a new balancing lake to reduce flooding. She has also persuaded the Labour Council to prepare for the new Renters Rights Act to make sure that private tenants will be able to get help from the Council from day 1 when it comes in in May. She has got free sessions on money advice and supported the service to advise on reducing energy consumption.

Locally she has tackled a wide variety of issues for residents from school places to housing assessments, litter and graffiti to blocked drains, major street light repairs, adopting land and tracing private owners to get their land maintained.

In her spare time she likes reading detective novels and works at fabric and fibre craft.

Uroy has lived in Milton Keynes for almost 40 years and has served as a Councillor for 11 years, currently representing Middleton and Oakgrove. Alongside his public service, he is the Cross-Campus Cluster Lead at UEL and Chair of City College London.

Locally, Uroy is a strong advocate for improving outcomes for vulnerable children. He is campaigning for more high-quality children’s homes for those in care, alongside better mental health support services. He believes that local people should be better supported to provide these services, rather than relying heavily on large external corporations.

Uroy works closely with community groups and residents and has tackled a wide range of local issues and addressing everyday concerns such as potholes, street lighting, graffiti, and fly-tipping.

Beyond his council work, Uroy is actively involved in his local church and is passionate about mentoring and supporting young people. As Shadow Cabinet Member for Equalities, Leisure and Culture, he has championed inclusive community initiatives and supported campaigns such as White Ribbon, promoting the protection and safety of women.

Macsene has lived in Milton Keynes for over 20 years. She is married with a teenage daughter, and works as a Process Engineering Consultant in the energy sector where she designs “green” technology add-ons for existing facilities.

She has been a Parish Councillor in Broughton and Milton Keynes Village Parish for over 5 years, where she works to help young people thrive.  This year she has helped to secure funding and access to mental health for young people in the Parish. She looks forward to helping young people across the ward as a city councillor.

She wants to work with residents of all ages to improve life in the community and has met with people at local warm spaces, parish events, litter picking and other community events. She has worked with Oakgrove School to reduce litter along the H7, and got streetlights and potholes fixed and graffiti, litter and fly-tipping removed from across Ouzel Valley ward.

In her spare time she likes to read and is trying to write a novel.

I am a resident of Peartree bridge, where I live with my family. I am passionate about Milton Keynes and especially, Ouzel Valley where my home is. I am standing to bring real change for this city and want especially to be an advocate and intermediary for my ward. Living in Ouzel Valley I am aware of all of the problems that we all face on a daily basis and in the last few weeks campaigning and knocking on doors I have become aware of many more. If elected I plan to concentrate all of my time addressing these issues and all of the concerns of the residents of Ouzel Valley ward.

Living in Monkston since 1999 with my family, I petitioned counsellors to keep a local nursery open and now I want to use my business experience to influence council decisions, to provide better value and services for you, to ensure that Milton Keynes remains a fantastic place to live and work.

As a husband and father, I’ve lived and worked in Buckinghamshire for over a decade. I’m standing to keep Ouzel Valley safe, clean, and thriving, supporting local businesses, protecting our countryside, and delivering practical solutions for residents.

*Hej du der!*

I’m Helene!

I'm a thoughtful and curious person, who believes in understanding issues properly before forming opinions. I value fairness, empathy, and critical thinking, and I try to approach decisions with both evidence and compassion. I try to remain particularly aware of people who may feel overlooked or unheard, and I believe politics should work for everyone, not just a few. I’m always open to learning and improving my views.

Campaign Priorities
Environment

I believe in carrot over stick, when it comes the enviornment. This means making it easier for people to choose sustainable options in their everyday lives.

This would include improving access to green and nature-friendly alternatives, and making walking and cycling safer and more practical. Issues such as bike theft, poor lighting, and uneven paths discourage people from choosing healthier and more sustainable ways of getting around. I want to focus on addressing these barriers so more people feel confident when traveling through our town.

I volunteer with food distribution, where I see firsthand how much good food goes to waste. At a time of rising living costs, environmental challenges, and public health concerns, reducing food waste and improving access to healthy food is essential. I believe local politicians should focus more on connecting surplus food with the people who need it.

Supporting Local Businesses

Small and local businesses are an important part of our community and create many local jobs. I want to make it easier to start and run a business by simplifying permits and reducing unnecessary bureaucracy.

I will work to support local entrepreneurship and ensure that the municipality is accessible and responsive to business owners. I also want to encourage diverse business ownership, so more people have the opportunity to succeed and contribute to our local economy.

Keeping local infrastructure like roads, bike paths and parks useable and safe.

Tabitha grew up in Stony Stratford, studied law at university and qualified as a barrister in 2009. She currently runs a small business locally.

She's passionate about fairness, problem solving and has experience doing casework for vulnerable members of society. Now her kids are older she’d like to work for the community that raised her.

Having been a Stony Stratford resident I care deeply about the town’s character, history & future. As your local councillor I will enhance what makes the town special, prevent over-development, ensure public money is used responsibly, address pot-holes and local infrastructure issues and support a safer community.

As a school caretaker for a number of years, a typical working man with strong values, I've seen how Milton Keynes' diverse community thrives when they are supported. I want a council that serves the law-abiding, tax-paying silent majority, creating the right conditions for all to thrive.

I've been an MK resident since our family moved here 48 years ago, and have been fortunate to live in Stony Stratford for the last 13 years.

With a work history that spans private sector datacentre management, local authority with transport and Public Health, and now charity sector with volunteering I have wide experience of how society and our communities work.

When not with the family i'm running, cycling, or volunteering with various other organisations - Stony Stratford Scouts, junior parkrun, The Stables, Walk Wheel Cycle Trust and others.

Campaign Priorities

I first moved to Milton Keynes in 1975, brought up my children here and witnessed huge changes, culminating in city status.
I’m passionate about preserving the character of Stony Stratford with its vibrant arts scene and independent businesses.

For too long, the constituency has been sidelined and neglected by those who were supposed to champion its needs. No more excuses. The people of this community deserve action, not just words—they deserve leadership that delivers rather than disappoints.

Shazna Muzammil is committed to turning these goals into reality because the residents deserve a home and a community that are cherished and safeguarded for future generations. Her plan, crafted by listening to the voices and concerns of the community, reflects a deep understanding of what needs to change. She is ready to champion these causes vigorously.

Her drive is fueled by the trust and support of the community, and she hopes to earn that trust and support to deliver real results.

Join Shazna, and let's work hard together to make our community a place we all feel PROUD to call home. She is dedicated to ensuring that every voice is heard and that every action taken is in the best interest of those she aims to serve.

I will listen to you, bring in the investment, and deliver the changes we desperately need here. We can rebuild our community where everyone feels secure and valued. It’s time for change.”

A Statement to Voters from James Lancaster – Milton Keynes Labour

Milton Keynes is our home, and Labour on the council has been working every day to make it stronger, fairer, and better for everyone who lives here.

We’ve protected what makes MK special. While others talk about cuts, Labour has stood up for our leisure centres, sports facilities, libraries, parks and cultural spaces — because we know they’re not “nice to have”, they’re the heart of our communities. They keep us healthy, bring us together, and give our kids opportunities to thrive.

This year’s manifesto is our best yet. It’s practical, ambitious, and built on what you’ve told us matters: safe neighbourhoods, affordable homes, good local jobs, and protecting the green spaces and services we all rely on. We’re backing grassroots sport, investing in culture, and making sure leisure stays accessible for every family, not just a few.

I’m James Lancaster, and I’m standing with Milton Keynes Labour to keep delivering. We’ve shown what we can do on the council — protecting services when others wouldn’t, listening to residents, and planning for MK’s future instead of short-term fixes.

On polling day, vote for a council that protects our leisure, sport and culture. Vote for a plan that puts Milton Keynes first. Vote James Lancaster and Milton Keynes Labour.

Why I am a good choice for the Tattenhoe Ward

Tattenhoe deserves a councillor who listens, acts, and puts residents first. That’s me!

I live locally and understand the issues we face every day — from protecting our green spaces and parks, to tackling speeding and parking problems, to making sure our estates get their fair share of investment. I won’t and don’t just turn up at election time. Im here all year round, knocking on doors, responding to casework, and standing up for Tattenhoe at the council.

With me, alongside Cllr’s Lancaster and O’Neill, you get someone who will fight to keep our community safe, clean, and connected. I back proper funding for youth services, sports facilities and community groups because i know they’re what hold a strong community together.

This election is about choosing someone who will be your voice, not just another vote. I feel I am rooted in this community and ready to work hard for it!

On polling day, choose a strong local champion. Vote Christian Durugo for Tattenhoe Ward!

I’m passionate about helping people, protecting green spaces & driving positive change in our community.

**Clare Tevlin** is an experienced **Liberal Democrat Councillor** in local government, first elected in 2022. In 2024, Clare stood as Parliamentary Candidate for Milton Keynes North. Shortly afterwards, Clare became an **MK City Councillor**, where she serves as **Shadow Cabinet Member for Adult Social Care and Housing**. Following the recent boundary changes for Milton Keynes, **Clare is standing for Walton Ward** for the **7 May 2026** City Council local election.

**Clare** has a background in policy, communications and project management.
As a Councillor, Clare has strong experience in **Planning, Scrutiny, Corporate Governance, Health, Communities and Partnership engagement**. Clare also serves as a director for an association for local councils, and recently completed leadership certification with the Institute for Leadership.

**Clare is community minded, puts residents first and gets the job done**. From Litter picking to reporting potholes, she has been **getting out and about in Walton Ward, engaging with residents**. Her passion is advocating for **safer communities for all**. Previous project work Clare has led on includes the raising awareness of and advocating against Child Exploitation, as well as projects around community development and infrastructure.

**Vote for Clare Tevlin, Liberal Democrat Focus Team, on 7 May**.

Tina Latifah Jones first moved to Milton Keynes back in 1996. Here, she has raised her daughters and fostered over twenty children, helping them through the most difficult and challenging times. I’ve lived in Loughton since 1987 and, with family ties to Cell Cottage, as mentioned in the Doomsday Book, I care deeply about our Ward. If elected, I’ll work hard to tackle local issues, preserve features of this wonderful Ward, use funding wisely, focusing on preventing overdevelopment.

**About**
I have lived in Loughton for 15 years. I love so much about Milton Keynes, especially our community, our diversity and our green spaces.

I am a qualified accountant. I was a school governor for 8 years, including 5 years as chair. In this role I worked to improve the outcomes for every child.

If elected, I would work for all Watling Ward residents to ensure their views are heard. I would aim to provide local services that give you the best value for your council tax payments.

**Campaign Priorities**
__Housing and development__

There should be affordable, sustainable housing for local people. New developments must have schools, shops, doctors, community centres, parks and public transport links as a priority.

__Green spaces__

We must maintain our parks, lakes and green spaces, ensuring they are accessible to everyone.

Mary Moore
About
Like many people in Milton Keynes, I was born and raised elsewhere, so living here is a choice. It's a choice I made thirteen years ago, and I've lived and worked here ever since.

For a large part of that time, I've been a union caseworker, so I'm used to fighting the corner for ordinary people. I'm used to listening to their concerns, finding solutions, and challenging authority when needed. I'm now asking the people of Watling ward to allow me to do the same for you, as your elected representative to Milton Keynes City Council.

Campaign Priorities
My campaign priorities are about ensuring we have a Council that serves you. One that has the needs and priorities of local people at the heart of everything it does. This includes:

An accessible and responsive Council that listens to local people.
Public services that benefit the public, not profiteers.
Common sense planning - ensuring new developments have the infrastructure needed to build their community. Not waiting years without a local shop or bus route, leaving people feeling isolated and unconnected.
Please let me be your voice on your Council.

Ansar Hussain is a former Mayor of Wolverton and is a well-known face across the ward! Ansar has served our community for well over a decade, first becoming a town councillor in 2012, and a city councillor in 2021. With a strong record of public service, Ansar is committed to fairness, and inclusive, effective representation.

Lauren Townsend lives in Wolverton with her young family, and can often be found at a local play park or MK Museum on the weekends. Lauren was first elected in 2019, and currently serves as Deputy Leader. She is known for being highly responsive and getting things done quickly and efficiently. In her day job, Lauren works for a trade union.

Learn more: https://www.mklabour.org.uk/may2026

Victoria Bamisile has served on Wolverton & Greenleys Town Council since 2020, and has been a city councillor since 2024. Passionate about children and young people, Victoria chairs this scrutiny committee on the city council. Victoria works hard for residents and is proud to stand up for Wolverton and its future.

Learn more: https://www.mklabour.org.uk/may2026

I am pleased to stand as Green Party candidate for Wolverton Ward in the city council elections on 7th May. I want to use my experiences as a professional Community Organiser, currently working for Cycling Citizens MK, to make decisions which help our city grow sustainably. I have enjoyed knocking on doors locally to get views about what needs to be done, and I am thankful for the support so many people across Wolverton Ward have shown.

Originally from London, I have lived nearly half my life in Wolverton, raising with my wife Kate three lovely children and making loads of friends in the area. I feel lucky. As a former governor of two local schools and long-time trustee of Old Bath House community centre, I know the area well. As a Green Party member since the 1980s, I hold closely the values of ecological wisdom and social justice. As an experienced Community Organiser, I have the skills to deliver change and can help the council achieve greater environmental sustainability and fairer health outcomes across the city.

I believe global warming and biodiversity loss are a real threat to us all and we must act together now - as citizens and consumers - so our children and theirs can live at least as safely as we have. Working for Cycling Citizens MK, the alliance of business, education and community organisations, I have seen a lack of councillor leadership on active travel. It’s not fair some people can’t get around easily. This is an opportunity to improve the physical and mental health of residents. I will do more to make this happen at ward and city level.

Nicky Kenny is passionate about plants and has spent many years gardening, growing and foraging. As an eighties child she spent her summer holidays fascinated by her grandfather’s garden, watching rows of dahlias which grew to the size of dinner plates! By the age of eighteen she became obsessed with houseplants and herbalism and has been growing these passions ever since. 
Nicky is also a keen walker and regularly leads guided heritage and foraging walks, sharing her love and knowledge of plants, learning from nature and the people she walks with, sharing an experience that supports health and wellbeing and enriches connections between people and place. 
Nicky is a keen photographer and is part of The Wolverton Society of Artists.

I have lived in Milton Keynes for four years working as a data analyst. I have been living in Wolverton for the last two years after living in Bletchley. To feel a connection to each of those areas makes me prouder than I ever thought it would, and I don't believe that our City gets anything like enough credit for our short yet significant history: the Open University which made higher education more accessible than ever before; Wolverton being the world's first purpose built railway town; our super efficient grid system and cycle paths; and of course Bletchley Park.

My priorities as a councillor would be to continue to build on our innovative history, all while putting the people of MK first. I just want people's lives to be easy, really. I will be ready to listen to anyone with mental or physical health issues on what I could do to help them as Councillor. I would also roll up my sleeves to get the practical stuff done - fixing the potholes springs to mind - because all our lives are busy enough that we dont need these extra worries and hassle.

It is time for England to do some more outside the box thinking and, all things considered, I believe that Milton Keynes is the place to start and The Green Party are the people to start it.

I live in Wolverton and work in the surrounding area. 
I feel wolverton is a forgotten corner of Milton keynes since the closure of the majority of the railway works. If elected, I'd fight for a fair share of funding to address both local issues and preserve & improve the features or our historic town.

Matt Hinton has lived in Milton Keynes for 35 years, much of that time spent working for the Open University. He believes we must balance new housing projects with preserving the historic character and green spaces in Wolverton. 
Matt is passionate about reforming social care with a strong focus on improving support for older adults, including those living with dementia, as well as supporting unpaid carers with respite care. Outside of work, he is a keen parkrunner.

I am a retired hospital doctor who is passionate about helping our community access the services it needs, especially in health and care. I have lived in Milton Keynes for 26 years and I love walking my two dogs through our city’s wonderful outdoor spaces.

Fed up with career politicians and empty promises? So am I. I ran my business in Kiln Farm for 35 years. Now retired I want to give the people of Wolverton a choice of someone to represent them who will listen and whose priority is serving the community.

My only promise: I will listen to you - drawing on
my experience to action / do whatever is possible.

As your Councillor I will:
Listen first - tell me what you need from the Council.
Fight for Wolverton and Milton Keynes.
Work my socks off - no spin, just action.

Donna Fuller is one of your hardworking local city councillors, and also serves
as Leader of Woughton Community Council. A well-known face around the Woughton & Fishermead
ward, Donna regularly holds surgeries and attends events like coffee mornings to meet and hear directly from you. She has raised her family here and is a proud mum and grandmother.

Amber McQuillan has lived in our ward for almost her entire life! She is a proud local resident who is now raising her own family here as a mum to seven wonderful children. She is passionate about making our area a better and more inclusive place for all children. One of her proudest achievements as your councillor is securing the inclusive play park on Coffee Hall.

Learn more: https://www.mklabour.org.uk/may2026

My name is Gary Toms, and I am proud to call Milton Keynes my home. Over the years, I have developed a strong connection to the local community, built on a genuine desire to help others and contribute in meaningful ways. Whether through small acts of support or more consistent involvement, I have always believed that even the smallest effort can make a positive difference in people’s lives.
Helping people has not just been something I do occasionally—it has been a consistent part of my life for many years. From assisting neighbours with everyday tasks to being available for those who need someone to listen, I have taken pride in being approachable, reliable, and willing to step in where I can. These experiences have shaped my understanding of community needs and strengthened my commitment to making a difference.
Through this ongoing involvement, I have gained valuable insight into the kinds of challenges people face on a daily basis. I understand that behind every issue, no matter how big or small, there are real people who deserve to be heard and supported. This awareness has inspired me to take my commitment a step further.
I am now hoping to stand for Milton Keynes Council. If selected, my goal is to represent the community with honesty, dedication, and a strong sense of responsibility. I want to ensure that local voices are listened to and that practical solutions are found for the issues that matter most to residents.
Ultimately, my aim is simple: to continue helping people, but on a larger scale. By working closely with the community and addressing concerns directly, I hope to contribute to a better, more supportive environment for everyone in Milton Keynes.

I have been a social worker, teacher and a school governor so I understand issues in relation to social care and education.

I am pro residents consultations and consent before decisions that affect them are made.

Having grown up just down the road in Bicester, I have now lived in Beanhill for almost 10 years. I work as an architect who specialises in low energy housing which removes the reliance on fossil fuels and reduces energy bills. My work also places emphasis on addressing the impact of climate change and improving biodiversity, working across Buckinghamshire, Hertfordshire, Oxfordshire, and London. I am also a committee member of the Royal Institute of British Architects Buckinghamshire branch.

Outside of architecture I can be found enjoying the green spaces Milton Keynes has to offer (including with a local dog walking group I help run), experimenting in my workshop building furniture, or working as an instructor for dog training classes.

**Campaign Priorities**

If elected I will listen to local residents - as they should be at the heart of all council decision making - and make sure funding goes to areas that matter to them. This will include getting potholes fixed, clearing up litter, maintaining the Redways, and improving the ward - which often feels overlooked by the council.

I will push for more help with the cost of living, including upgrades to housing to help lower energy bills.

I love Milton Keynes and think the city is something special. We have beautiful green spaces alongside the workings of a modern city, but Woughton & Fishermead deserves better than it's currently receiving. It’s time to try something different. The Green Party can provide much needed change.

My email address is catpaynemk@gmail.com if you would like to get in contact.

**About**

I’ve lived, participated in and experienced the development of Milton Keynes since the first master plan was published in 1967. I’m a massive enthusiast for this city and believe it to be one of the best places to live anywhere. Having lived on Netherfield for many years I worked with Langland school pupils to create a mural on the shopping arcade. I am currently treasurer of my local environmental group and see great value in grass roots volunteer projects, such as litter picking. As a keen cyclist I appreciate the benefits of a safe, well-maintained cycling network running through quiet parklands and better public transport to tempt people out of their cars.

**Campaign Priorities**

The preservation, good management and improvement of cycleways and green spaces
Affordable housing
Arts, music and education services 
Support for young adults and youth culture 
Sustainable ecology and economics with fact- based policy and fair minded decision making.
Fixing potholes!

I’m a Councillor at Woughton CC and have a strong record of working with local people to get problems fixed. I love the community spirit of our neighbourhoods and can often be seen working to protect our local environment for future generations.

I’m standing as an Independent to give local people a stronger voice and to make sure the priorities of residents in Woughton & Fishermead are properly heard and acted on.

I’ve been a family carer for many years, so I understand first-hand the pressures many people face. I also support other carers to access the help they need. I care deeply about fairness — whether that’s improving access to social housing, strengthening renters’ rights, or making sure everyone has better opportunities in education.

Locally, I have a track record of getting things done. Working with residents and other independents, I’ve helped tackle issues like fly-tipping and broken street lighting. I want to see safer, cleaner streets and redways, better access to green spaces, and more opportunities for young people.

Alongside this, I work in education, helping to bring in funding and develop projects that support fairness and opportunity for communities like ours. I’ve also worked on initiatives that promote practical action on the environment — focusing on making our area cleaner, greener, and healthier without losing sight of everyday concerns.

I’m committed to standing up for all seven estates in Woughton & Fishermead and making sure our community is not overlooked.

I’ve lived in Milton Keynes for 40 years, working in partnership with council officers to improve safety and health for women and girls. I’m a regular bus user and also campaign to make cycling easier for all. As an independent councillor, I can be accountable to residents of Woughton and Fishermead, and prioritise their needs without being tied to a party line.

I am Jeannie Bartrick. I moved to Springfield in Milton Keynes in 2024 from St Neots and am married to James
I worked as a High School teacher and Head of Department in Social Sciences for 20 years before I retired a couple of years ago. The students were aged 11 to 18 years. The role also included handling budgets, liaising with other professionals externally and within the academy. It also involved creating school improvement plans and seeing these through to fruition. During this time I was also involved in teacher training. Prior to that I was an Early Years support advisor working in Norfolk supporting 60 Early Years setting in schools and private nurseries. 
SInce we moved to MK we have visited Fishermead regularly as our Doctor's surgery and Pharmacy is there. While appreciating the multicultural nature of the ward, I would look to ensure all residents are equally supported. It is clearly a deprived area and I aim to identify and support the much needed areas for improvement.
My priority if elected would be to try and help families across the ward. Given my education background it would be a priority to get better access to SEND support.
Having been to Tinkers Bridge and Coffee Hall, there are clearly housing issues which need working on. Working with resident groups to get major repairs done would also be a priority.

I’m standing for the Liberal Democrats in Woughton & Fishermead because I believe this area deserves councillors who understand real life, not just council paperwork.
I’m an NHS doctor working in respiratory and intensive care, so I see every day how housing, poverty, isolation, poor transport, unsafe streets and lack of opportunity affect people’s health. For me, politics is not abstract. It is about whether someone gets their damp fixed, whether an older person feels safe walking home, whether a young person has somewhere positive to go, and whether families feel listened to before decisions are made about their neighbourhood.
I’m also  a husband, a homeowner and someone who knows what it means to work hard, build a life, and still feel that the system does not always listen. I did not come from privilege or political connections. I know the value of persistence, graft and showing up.
That is the kind of councillor I would be: visible, practical and persistent.
My priorities are straightforward:
- Cleaner estates.
- Safer streets.
- Better housing and repairs.
- More support for young people.
- A stronger focus on health inequality.
- Councillors who actually listen, act and report back.
I am not standing to make noise for the sake of it. I am standing because Woughton & Fishermead need serious, energetic representation from people who care about the basics and are prepared to chase them properly.
If you want a councillor who will be visible, who understands public service, and who will fight for a fairer, safer and healthier Woughton & Fishermead, I would be grateful for your vote.
